bead.title: Add template-conformance validate mode to HELIX routing skill
bead.description:
skills/helix/SKILL.md currently has align (drift/gaps), evolve (thread changes), and check (next-action). It lacks an explicit mode for validating an artifact instance against its own template's prompt and structural shape — the dogfood loop described in PRD R-3 (alignment skill output) and R-6 (self-application).

Add a validate route to the routing table and a corresponding contract. Contract shape (mirroring the existing route contracts):

1. Load the artifact instance and resolve its artifact type (via ddx: frontmatter when present, else by path/filename pattern).
2. Load the template.md, prompt.md, and meta.yml for that artifact type from workflows/phases/*/artifacts/<type>/.
3. Compare instance to template structurally (required section headings present, required frontmatter fields populated) and compare instance content to prompt.md guidance (each prompt section answered or explicitly marked N/A).
4. Emit findings keyed to template/prompt sections, classified as ALIGNED, INCOMPLETE, UNDERSPECIFIED, or STALE per existing align taxonomy.
5. Produce updates (apply directly when the user invoked validate to fix, or surface a plan when they invoked it to audit).

Add 'validate' to the routing table with intent 'Check an artifact instance against its template and prompt; improve in place'. Keep the contract body under ~25 lines, matching the density of existing routes.
bead.acceptance: skills/helix/SKILL.md contains a 'validate' route in the Routing Rules table and a Validate section under Workflow Contracts. The contract names: artifact-type resolution; template+prompt+meta.yml load; structural and prompt-section conformance checks; finding classification; produce updates. Skill body remains runtime-neutral (no ddx/helix CLI commands in normative body). A grep for 'validate' in skills/helix/SKILL.md finds the new section.
